examples: The great thinker who interprets the Christian religion in terms of eros is St. Augustine.
He thought that God was agape. For him, that was the basis of Christianity. But Eros thought of the matter what St.Augustine said is selfish.
First of all, Aristotle derived the term philia from Plato's eros, and he redefined love as something that is more dependent on reason than on passion.
Yellow roses, signifying friendship more than eros, seemed right, given the complex potentials of the evening.
Eros was named by German astronomer Gustav Witt, who discovered the asteroid in 1898.
